text: There is some uncertainty about the system on which the Romans computed the years of a given reign; probably the fifteenth year of Tiberius would be 28 or 29a.d. by our reckoning.
Caiphas was the actual high priest; Annas, who had been deposed from that office, continued to exercise much influence.
The soldiers were perhaps those on guard at the customs houses; the temptations they are to resist would be, in the modern world, those of the police.
